Competent assessment and management at much higher level than a PGY1.

This is an important feature to understand the functional demands and functional deficits of this patient., and helps to guide further investigations.

column35: History taking was effective as it was directed with specific questions about duration of symptoms/timeline and family history , good rapport with patient and able to have a conversation while taking key history. In terms of approach to investigations and management, overall had a good understanding of approaching colon cancer. My suggestion is to work on summarising and structure as Sunny has lots of knowledge. This can be done by undertaking further cases and presenting/discussing cases.

column32: History ,Physical Examination ,Management/Counselling ,Clinical Judgement ,Communication Skills,Patient Safety and Quality of Care
column34: Short history of abdominal pain and dysuria. Primary carer for patient in ED, completing full workup of history, exam, investigation and surgical referral. Identified appendicitis.
column35: Thorough assessment with consideration of important differentials such as testicular torsion. Clear and well structured. Inclusion/consideration of communication with patient and family throughout. For note-keeping, needs to remember to reproduce date and time on each page when notes extend to a new piece of paper.

column34: Lady on oxygen with severe pulmonary hypertension and COPD who has refractory fluid overload, obesity
column35: Able to clearly explain the issues regarding management of oedema in this patient and how he would approach this including use of frusemide infusion, mentioned oral medications but could have expanded on the use if sequential nephron blockade. Did not comment on drowsiness of patient although he was able to converse with her. Was nervous.

column34: Febrile while on lymphoma treatment
column35: Adequate for intern level. For a better assessment I would suggest including patient’s unique situation to personalise their plan. Eg history of IE and lymphoma. Previous culture results.

column34: Presented with abdominal pain and vomitting, uti
column35: Polite, good listening skills and empathetic. Could be strengthened with addressing relevant negatives and some further detail on some questions but had solid structure to develop.

column34: Major Depressive Disorder (melancholia)
column35: Good history covering all domains needed with excellent differentials Able to engage a difficult to engage person

column34: Syncope , Falls
column35: 1. Nutritional Assessment needs detailing 2. Possibility of Aortic Stenosis needs to be mentioned

column34: 65 y old female with cecal cancer with obstruction for which a lap right hemicolectomy was done. Postoperative day 3 examination
column35: Systematic assessment of patient presentation and clinical exam was thorough.

column34: Abnormal uterine bleeding and chronic pelvic pain
column35: Professional behaviour was appreciable. He needs to be more objective in history taking and develop focus on facts relevant to presentation.

column34: G4P3 at 33+6/40 with UTI
column35: Good counseling skill , but need to strengthen the skill of eliciting risk factors in the history.

column34: Foot drop following L THR
column35: Organised, sensible and thorough assessment. Appropriate management plan.

column34: First episode syncope, unclear if cardiogenic or neurogenic. Recent description of subjective fevers and contact with bovine as part of his occupation. Collateral history available during assessment
column35: Subjective fever and contact with bovine should prompt consideration of zoonotic diseases. Clear attempts to identify red flags. Good outline of considered investigations. Good utilisation of available collateral history

column34: Severe depression/ anxiety in context of death of Uncle- executor of will & carer for elderly parents . Overwhelmed by stress, responsibility & grief. Symptoms “everyday all day”. Constant rumination & somatic symptoms. Guilt- ridden. Attempted treatment in community, but suicidal ideation heightened.
column35: Good introduction & explanation of intention/ confidentiality. Calm manner. Readily engaged patient. Open ended questions. Reassuring & empathic. Established symptoms. Good time management.

column32: Management/Counselling ,Clinical Judgement ,Patient Safety and Quality of Care
column34: 16 weeks pregnant with major foetal abnormalities requiring medical termination of pregnancy in hospital. Uncomplicated process
column35: Very familiar with the medicine in this case. Excellent clear explanations and demonstration of understanding of non clinical issues. It will be interesting to learn, over time, more about different cultures' health beliefs and attitudes. highly competent in this assessment

column34: 75 years old man who presents with 3 months of lethargy, insomnia and frequent falls following a recent blood test with the GP. This blood test indicated he had hyponatraemia.
column35: Good introduction to patient. Showed empathy and allowed patient to talk. History taking was comprehensive but not well organised. Focus on the presenting complaint/reason first before delving into a systems review. Always latch on to the hints and information that is provided by the patients that you see as relevant and explore that first before moving on. Able to rationalise relevant investigations. Be specific about the reasons for your tests without requiring prompting.

column34: Schizophrenia. Psychosis drug induced. Lengthy stay. On depot.
column35: Excellent interview. Easy engagement / good rapport established. Asked all relevant questions in relaxed manner.

column34: Older patient presenting with symptomatic anaemia related to drug induced gastric injury. Complexity presyncope, heart disease, age
column35: Covered all possible contributors to and risks from this condition. No deficiencies, excellent whole of patient perspective

column34: Newly diagnosed diabetes in DKA Heeadsss assessment
column35: Took a focused history on a diabetic patient and then took a full paediatric history very well with attention to using the HEEADSSS tool

column34: Post menopausal bleeding
column35: Comprehensive history and investigation plan

column34: RIGHT knee complex PJI with recurrence if infection.
column35: Good assessment. Well organised investigation and management plan.

column32: History ,Physical Examination ,Management/Counselling ,Clinical Judgement ,Cultural Competence,Patient Safety and Quality of Care
column34: Prolonged history of LUTS minimally improved with antibiotics. Presenting to ED with fevers and right loin pain. Thorough history (including appropriate gynaecological history to consider PID). Patient lives rurally (90km from regional centre), without a regular GP.
column35: Well-considered history, thorough with appropriate focus and clear consideration of differentials as progressing through history and examination. The differential list was a bit too long (after 3rd point they were becoming much less relevant), and could be curtailed simply to 'most likely' and 'major differential'. Good selection of investigations, appropriately followed up and documented. Appropriately seeking senior review and utilising resources (abx guidelines).

column33: History,Physical examination,Management/Counselling ,Clinical judgement,Communication skills,Working in a team,Professionalism,Cultural competence,Patient safety and quality of care
column34: Right renal colic with possible pyelonephritis; first episode and currently undifferentiated
column35: High level performance for a common ED presentation; good differential diagnosis and rationale; comprehensive investigation approach and initial ED management strategies; slow down in real life 😀

column34: 11 month old boy admitted for bronchiolitis requiring high flow oxygen
column35: She was very professional towards mother and pleasant to patient. She was organised with her counselling and provided important details regarding the diagnosis and management in hospital and post-discharge.

column34: Patient with vascular disease and postural drop of 50mmHg who presented with a fall, to discuss approach to management and prescribing of this condition
column35: Good rapport with the patient. Spoke clearly and gave clear descriptions of her approach to the patient. Discussed the general approach to the condition( remove BP lowering meds and rehydrate) but besides asking him to get up slowly did not offer any other approaches such as increasing salt intake,or considering Ted stockings, seemed unaware of medications that might help, did say she would consult other colleagues to aid in her management, and she may not have seen this problem before

column34: 25 year old lady referred to Scone Emergency Department from GP rooms with headache not responding to simple analgesia / 'migraine specific' treatment. GP concern for meningitis. Assessment of patient in Scone ED by Dr Kamal with normal vital signs and examination and no features concerning for serious / secondary headache cause.
column35: Thorough history, examination, and differential list. Well considered and correctly identified that no sinister headache pathology was likely. There could be more demonstration of this clinical reasoning in the record keeping. Suggest that if a patient leaves prior to our recommended investigations, documentation needs to address the risk of this. In this case there was no serious risk, though that could also be documented. Otherwise well done and thorough and good demonstration of senior help.

column34: Delusional Disorder with deterioration in context of cessation of medication
column35: Good interview with tangential patient. Assessed risks appropriately beyond basics of self harm, to look at the wider risk profile. Able to direct patient when they were going off topic

column34: D4 prostatectomy for prostate cancer
column35: Great patient manner and communication. Patient at ease during history and physical exam. Structured and logical approach taken to assess post op patient. Identified key findings of distended abdomen and erythematous wound. Only minor improvements suggested -summarising pertinent findings when presenting patient case; enhancing assessment by using stethoscope to listen for bowel sounds. Specific actions include practising exam on surgical patients and summarising cases during her ED rotation.

column34: G1P0 at 37 weeks with Gestational hypertension
column35: Comprehensive history and management plan. Detailed examination

column34: 7 year old with chronic asthma with acute asthma presentation
column35: Took a focused asthma history identifying trigger and risk factors and gave appropriate counselling

column34: Fall at home. Hip pain, head strike on NOAC. Cardiac issues with recent pacemaker insertion.
column35: Just competent. We discussed holes in the history taking that were important, especially recent pacemaker, medications and function. Also appropriate bedside and other tests - missed doing an ECG.

column34: 59M presented with chest pain. Background of previous MI requiring balloonoplasty with similar symptoms.
column35: A well structured and efficient history and examination. Very good knowledge demonstrated during discussion around management of ACS and modification to hypothetical inferior/ RV infarct pathology.

column34: Lower abdominal pain with increased PV discharge for 1 week post random sexual encounter. past 3 months had 5 sexual partners, nil contraception or barrier protection used. smokes / drinks / also has pea shapped swelling in left labia. G2P1 Top1 previosu hx of sti and genital herpes. O/E with chaperone, abdo snt, milking white discharge. Impression 1 - likely STI - mx with appropriate abx, follow up with GP,contacting of sexual partners to be done with GP. 2 - Bartholian cyst and at risk behavi
column35: Very good history, covered others that could be involved in case, aware this may be mental health concern as well and will get follow up with GP. Followed guidelines regarding management of suspected sti. spoke well with patient. Improvement - child - who is looking after her when sexual encounters are occuring, is she being subjected to random men in the house, is she at risk?

column34: 16 year old girl who presents after deliberately overdosing on dexamphetamine with a background of suicidal ideation. Clinically had tachycardia and agitation.
column35: Good documentation of a very thorough and comprehensive history involving 2 systems which were toxicological and mental health. Good rationalisation of clinical assessment and investigations in order to rule out other differentials. Suggest documenting time, date and name to every page on the medical records. Suggest to also make a note of differentials being considered at the time of assessment.

column34: Post op carpal tunnel with ongoing symptoms
column35: Did well, good assessment and management plan

column34: First episode seizure in an immunocompromised patient with background of bilateral lung transplant on Tacrolimus, Azathioprine, Prednisone, Bactrim, Valacyclovir. Appropriate initial seizure control established with further antiepileptic medications administered.
column35: Keppra loading could be higher. Good exploration and consideration of antimicrobial coverage. Good differentials for first episode seizure. Appropriate first seizure discharge plan.

column34: 15 year old with urinary incontinence and now coming with urinary retention, previous seizure activity
column35: Good communication, covered the basic questions and discussed necessary investigations

column34: 71 yo male with a background of cardiac surgery, hypertension, diabetes mellitus who has been admitted with hypotension and fever. Examination with regards to cause of fever
column35: Unfortunately for the purposes of a physician examination with regards to fever this examination was very superficial and limited. There was minimal exposure for eg when examining the abdomen and legs there was no exposure, no attempt to examine joints, mouth and there were multiple missing multiple examination findings including janeway lesions. This candidate unfortunately also could not identify that the patient had mechanical mitral and aortic valves

column34: Post op after right hemicolectomy
column35: Good systematic approach to evaluation. A bit rushed/ anxious but this is not really a problem.
